name: Asón Valley Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – running circular a la cascada del Asón
description: This moderate 4.0-mile (6.5 km) jogging route in Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón leads to the impressive Cascada del Asón.
region_intro: Jogging routes in Collados Del Asón traverse a landscape shaped by significant karstic and glacial processes, featuring impressive limestone outcrops and glacial circuses. The region offers a mix of small beech forests, unique Cantabrian oak groves, and expansive meadows. The Asón River, with its dramatic waterfall, carves through canyons, providing varied terrain for runners. Cantabrian summits within the park offer opportunities for more strenuous climbs and panoramic views. Best jogging routes in Collados Del Asón The most popular jogging route is Asón Valley Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views along the Asón River valley. Another top favourite among local runners is Asón Waterfall loop from Asón, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route features the stunning Asón River waterfall and scenic natural surroundings. Local runners also love the Brenarromán – Canal Honda loop from Soba, an 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail leading through challenging terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 52 minutes. Jogging in Collados Del Asón is defined by karstic formations, lush forests, and river valleys. The extensive network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Collados Del Asón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Collados Del Asón?
A: Collados Del Asón offers 14 dedicated running trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Collados Del Asón?
A: The jogging routes in Collados Del Asón feature a unique blend of karstic and glacial formations, including impressive limestone outcrops and glacial circuses. You'll run through small beech forests, Cantabrian oak groves, and expansive meadows, with varied terrain ranging from wide paths to more challenging mountainous ascents.

Q: Are there options for different fitness levels among the running routes?
A: Yes, Collados Del Asón provides a good mix of routes. There are 7 moderate trails perfect for a steady run, and 7 difficult routes for those seeking a more challenging experience with significant elevation gains. This variety ensures options for both casual joggers and experienced trail runners.

Q: Can I find routes that feature waterfalls in Collados Del Asón?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall loop from Asón&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate path that takes you directly to the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the source of the Asón River. The river itself is a central feature of many routes, offering picturesque views and the refreshing sound of cascading water.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while running in the park?
A: Collados Del Asón is rich in natural beauty. Along your runs, you can discover landmarks such as the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4347749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Posadía&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4338843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Honda&lt;/a&gt; gorge. Other notable natural features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4902310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brenarromán&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4339486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Bezón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Collados Del Asón?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular paths, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall loop from Asón&lt;/a&gt; and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24573917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brenarromán – Canal Honda loop from Soba&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a substantial workout with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging trails in Collados Del Asón Natural Park?
A: Collados Del Asón Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's important to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations to preserve the park's natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Collados Del Asón?
A: The park offers a beautiful setting year-round. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for running. Summer can be warm, but shaded forest paths offer relief. Winter runs are possible, but some higher elevation trails might be affected by snow or ice, so checking conditions beforehand is advisable.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running paths suitable for children?
A: While the guide focuses on jogging routes, many of the moderate trails, particularly those along river valleys or through meadows, can be adapted for family walks or runs with older children. The tranquil environment and diverse ecosystems make for an engaging outdoor experience. Always check the route's distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the jogging trails in Collados Del Asón?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20159816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Asón Waterfall loop from Asón&lt;/a&gt;, you can typically find parking in or near the village of Asón. For other routes, such as those starting from Soba or Arredondo, local parking areas are usually available. It's recommended to check specific route details for the most convenient parking options.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Collados Del Asón?
A: Runners frequently praise the undisturbed natural environment and the rich variety of ecosystems. The peacefulness allows for immersion in nature, and the stunning glacial and karstic landscapes, dramatic waterfalls, and lush forests create a truly exceptional backdrop for an invigorating run. The extensive and varied trail network also receives high marks for catering to different preferences.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the circular running route to Cascada del Asón?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact circular running route are not detailed, access to the Cascada del Asón typically involves parking near the starting points of trails within the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón. Look for designated parking areas in villages or near trailheads that lead towards the waterfall.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – running circular to la cascada del Asón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, natural parks in Spain allow dogs, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check for specific local regulations or signage at the trailhead regarding dogs within the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are highly recommended for running this route. During these seasons, the Asón Waterfall typically has a strong water flow, and the surrounding forests display vibrant colors. The weather is also generally more pleasant for jogging than in summer or winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón or its trails, including this running route. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this running route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this circular running route is varied. You'll encounter a mix of earth paths, grassy sections, and stony areas. Some parts may be muddy, especially after rain, and closer to the river. Expect forest tracks, bridle paths, and potentially irregular or steeper sections, particularly as you approach the Asón Waterfall. Sure-footedness is recommended.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and sights along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the spectacular Asón Waterfall (Cascada del Asón), a 70-meter cascade. Along the way, you'll follow the Asón River, pass through charming forests of beeches, chestnuts, and oaks, and cross meadows where livestock graze. The route also showcases unique karstic geological formations, limestone outcrops, and glaciated valleys.

faqs.question: How difficult is this circular running route to Cascada del Asón?
faqs.answer: This 6.5 km circular running route with approximately 230 meters of elevation gain is considered moderate. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness due to the varied terrain, which can include stony, muddy, and sometimes steep sections. It's a rewarding challenge for trail runners.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route partially overlaps with sections of other official trails. You will encounter parts of the CIMA CA08 Asón - La Sía, the Camino del Val del Asón (Nacimiento del río Asón), and the Camino a la cascada del nacimiento del río Asón, as well as segments of the GR-74.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while running in the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón?
faqs.answer: The park is home to diverse wildlife. Along the river, you might spot aquatic birds like the dipper and kingfisher, and if you're very lucky, the elusive Iberian desman or Atlantic salmon. The forests and meadows also support various other species, though many are shy.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for running this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, including potentially stony, muddy, and steep sections, sure-footedness is important. Wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Be mindful of livestock in meadows. The trails are generally well-marked with white and yellow PR-S signs, but it's always wise to carry a map or GPS device.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: While rated as moderate, a 6.5 km route with 230 meters of elevation gain might be challenging for absolute beginners. If you're new to trail running, ensure you have a reasonable level of fitness and are comfortable with uneven surfaces. Consider starting with shorter, flatter sections if available, or be prepared to walk some of the steeper or more technical parts.
